description: Applied Stoic practices for daily living: the three disciplines, preferred indifferents, cosmopolitan ethics, and reflective training loops.
ethical_guardrail: Use for self-governance and ethical action; never to justify emotional suppression, tolerate abuse, or dismiss injustice—safety and boundaries override 'acceptance'.

rules.name: Discipline of Desire (Orexis): Want What Nature Allows
rules.principle: Suffering drops when you train desire toward virtue and what is within your control, not toward outcomes you can't command.
detection_triggers: I want this outcome so badly it's ruining me
If this doesn't happen, I'm done
I can't accept reality as it is
I'm chasing something I can't control
How do I stop wanting what I can't have?
rules.insight_template: When {situation} triggers craving or aversion, apply orexis by shifting desire from {external_outcome} to {virtuous_aim}. Write: 'I prefer {preferred_result}, but I choose {virtuous_aim} regardless,' then take {next_action} that is fully under your control.

rules.name: Discipline of Action (Horme): Reserved Action
rules.principle: Act vigorously on what you control while reserving outcomes to reality; you commit to effort, not to guarantees.
detection_triggers: I won't start unless I'm sure it works
If I fail, it will be humiliating
I'm afraid to try because the outcome isn't certain
I keep waiting for perfect conditions
How do I act without needing control of results?
rules.insight_template: When {goal} feels risky, apply reserved action by committing to {process_action} and releasing {outcome}. Say: 'I will do {process_action}; if {setback} happens, I will respond with {contingency}.' Then execute the smallest step today to reduce inertia.

rules.name: Discipline of Assent (Synkatathesis): Full Impression Audit
rules.principle: Between impression and response is the power to test the story; assent only to judgments that survive scrutiny.
detection_triggers: I instantly assume the worst
My mind tells a story and I believe it
I get triggered and act without thinking
I keep interpreting everything as an attack
How do I stop believing my first thought?
rules.insight_template: When {trigger_event} hits, apply synkatathesis by running a 3-step audit: (1) Impression: 'I notice {raw_fact}'; (2) Story: 'My mind says {story}'; (3) Test: 'What evidence for/against?' Assent only to {tested_claim}, then choose {wise_response} aligned with values.

rules.name: Preferred Indifferents: Use Them Without Worship
rules.principle: Health, wealth, and reputation are 'preferred' but not moral goods; pursue them with virtue and drop them without collapse.
detection_triggers: If I lose my status, I'm nothing
Money is the only thing that matters
I can't handle being disliked
I need this promotion to be okay
How do I care without being controlled by it?
rules.insight_template: When {indifferent} starts to control you, apply preferred indifferents by separating preference from identity: 'I prefer {indifferent}, but my good is {virtue}.' Set a clean goal for {indifferent_goal} plus a virtue constraint {virtue_constraint}, then take {next_action} that honors both.

rules.name: Sympatheia: Act as Part of the Whole
rules.principle: You are a node in a larger system; wise action considers interdependence and the common good.
detection_triggers: I feel isolated and disconnected
Why should I care about other people?
Everyone is selfish so I should be too
I want to act with compassion but not be naive
How do I balance self-interest and the common good?
rules.insight_template: When {relationship} feels adversarial, apply sympatheia by naming shared reality: 'We are both affected by {shared_constraint}.' Choose {prosocial_action} that improves the system for everyone while keeping {boundary}. This is cooperation with clarity, not self-abandonment.

rules.name: Eudaimonia vs Hedonia: Choose Flourishing Over Hits
rules.principle: Pleasure is a side-effect; flourishing comes from virtue, meaning, and consistent right action.
detection_triggers: I'm chasing dopamine and it's not working
I keep scrolling/eating/escaping
I want a meaningful life, not just comfort
This pleasure wears off fast
How do I stop living for quick hits?
rules.insight_template: When {temptation} offers short-term relief, apply eudaimonia by asking: 'Does this build {character_strength} and long-term {life_aim}?' Replace {temptation} with {higher_action} that serves your values, and schedule {small_reward} after the work to avoid burnout.

rules.name: The Sage as Compass: Direction, Not Perfection
rules.principle: The Sage is an orientation device—use the ideal to steer choices without turning it into self-attack.
detection_triggers: I'm not stoic enough
I keep failing at this and feel ashamed
If I can't do it perfectly, why try?
I judge myself for being emotional
How do I practice without perfectionism?
rules.insight_template: When {self_criticism} spikes, apply the Sage-as-compass: define the next 1-degree improvement toward {virtue} (not a total overhaul). Ask: 'What would the Sage do in the next 5 minutes?' Then do {micro_action} and record it as a win in {log}.

rules.name: Stoa Poikile: Learn in Public, Train with Others
rules.principle: Stoicism is strengthened by community: dialogue, role-modeling, accountability, and shared practice.
detection_triggers: I keep trying alone and falling off
I need accountability
I want mentors or a community for this
Reading isn't translating into behavior
How do I practice this with other people?
rules.insight_template: When {practice} stalls, apply the Stoa Poikile principle by adding social structure: join {community}, schedule {cadence} check-ins, and do one 'public commitment' to {habit}. Use steelmanning in discussions and log one lesson per session.

rules.name: Premeditatio Malorum Extended: Pre-Mortem for Life
rules.principle: Imagining realistic setbacks plus response plans converts anxiety into preparedness and reduces surprise.
detection_triggers: I'm worried everything will go wrong
I can't sleep because I'm thinking of disasters
What if the plan fails?
I'm anxious about the future
How do I prepare without spiraling?
rules.insight_template: When {plan} feels fragile, apply extended premeditation: list 3 likely setbacks ({setback_1}, {setback_2}, {setback_3}), write a response for each, and add a buffer {margin_of_safety}. Then pick one prevention step {prevention_action} you can do this week.

rules.name: Journaling as Philosophical Practice: Morning + Evening
rules.principle: Writing clarifies judgments, reveals patterns, and turns philosophy into a feedback loop you can improve.
detection_triggers: My thoughts are chaotic
I want a journaling practice that works
I keep repeating the same patterns
I need more self-awareness
How do I reflect without overthinking?
rules.insight_template: When {mind_state} is noisy, apply philosophical journaling: morning—set {intention} and one virtue focus {virtue}; evening—review {event}, note where you assented too fast, and choose {adjustment} for tomorrow. Keep it to {minutes} minutes to avoid rumination.

rules.name: Physics as Foundation for Ethics: Live in Accord with Nature
rules.principle: Understanding how reality works (change, limits, causality) stabilizes judgment and grounds ethics in the possible.
detection_triggers: This isn't fair and I can't accept it
I keep fighting reality
Why do bad things happen randomly?
I want to be calm in uncertainty
How do I ground my values in reality?
rules.insight_template: When {event} feels unjust or chaotic, apply Stoic physics by naming the governing forces in {system} (entropy, delays, constraints). Accept the fact-pattern, then choose {ethical_action} that is feasible under those constraints. Reality sets the board; virtue chooses the move.

rules.name: Cosmopolitan Identity: Citizen of the World
rules.principle: You can hold firm boundaries while treating every person as part of the same human community.
detection_triggers: I hate people who disagree with me
They're the enemy
Society feels tribal and toxic
I want to be principled without being cruel
How do I stay humane in conflict?
rules.insight_template: When {conflict} turns tribal, apply cosmopolitan identity: treat the other as a fellow citizen. Separate the person from the idea, state {principle_boundary} clearly, and choose {respectful_action} that preserves dignity. You can reject behavior without rejecting humanity.

CART-STOIC: Extends Stoic fundamentals into the full three-disciplines framework and community-based daily practice loops.
CART-UNIVERSAL-LAWS: Uses universal laws (entropy, inertia, delays, constraints) as Stoic 'physics' for reality-aligned ethics.
CART-KAHNEMAN: Debiasing support: many failures of assent/desire are System 1 hijacks (present bias, loss aversion).
CART-EPISTEMIC: Epistemic tools strengthen assent (falsification, calibration) and prevent denial-as-stoicism.
